narrative: Ethereum and solana outpaced bitcoin by a wide margin today — ETH up 4.72% and SOL up 5.67% vs BTC's 1.74% — as the Bank of Japan's 25bp rate hike to a 31-year high triggered a risk-on reaction rather than the carry-trade unwind traders had feared per CoinDesk. Sentiment turned visibly bullish: Standard Chartered's Geoffrey Kendrick declared "crypto spring is here" and Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong said bitcoin likely bottomed near $60,000, while Strategy added 1,587 BTC and Bitmine added 76,881 ETH in the same session per CoinDesk. An ETF divergence emerged: bitcoin funds bled cash (driven by Grayscale's GBTC) while ether, XRP, solana, and Hyperliquid ETFs all attracted inflows per CoinDesk.
worth_watching: XRP — rallied 10% to $1.25 before giving back gains on profit-taking; not on watchlist but ETF inflows and Asian demand are supporting each recovery attempt per CoinDesk
Kraken US perpetual futures — launched today after CFTC approval, moving the 'crypto's next ETF moment' from prediction to live product; institutional adoption timeline has started
Bitcoin/altcoin ETF divergence — GBTC drove BTC ETF outflows while ETH, SOL, XRP, and Hyperliquid ETFs all gained per CoinDesk; a rotation story worth tracking to see if it's structural or one-day
